  • Hide All Code

Sakila Database Analysis

Eugenia Bening (M0724025)

2026-05-26

1 Pendahuluan

Latar Belakang

Database Sakila adalah contoh basis data MySQL yang merepresentasikan sistem persewaan film (DVD rental store). Database ini mencakup informasi tentang film, pelanggan, staf, pembayaran, dan transaksi rental. Dalam analisis ini, kami mengeksplorasi pendapatan rental berdasarkan kategori genre film serta tren pendapatan bulanan untuk memahami pola bisnis penyewaan film.

1.1 Deskripsi Insight

Insight yang diambil dalam analisis ini adalah:

“Bagaimana distribusi pendapatan rental film berdasarkan kategori genre, dan bagaimana tren pendapatan bulanan pada toko penyewaan film Sakila?”

Pertanyaan analitik yang akan dijawab:

  1. Genre film apa yang menghasilkan pendapatan terbesar?
  2. Bagaimana tren pendapatan dari bulan ke bulan?
  3. Apakah ada korelasi antara jumlah rental dan total pendapatan per genre?
  4. Bagaimana distribusi rating film berpengaruh terhadap volume rental?

1.2 Variabel yang Digunakan

Tabel Variabel Deskripsi
payment amount, payment_date Jumlah pembayaran dan tanggal
rental rental_id, rental_date ID dan tanggal rental
inventory inventory_id, film_id Relasi inventori ke film
film title, rating, rental_rate Detail film
film_category category_id Relasi film ke kategori
category name Nama kategori/genre

2 Persiapan Data

2.1 Instalasi & Loading Package

packages <- c("RMySQL", "DBI", "dplyr", "ggplot2", "tidyr", "lubridate",
               "scales", "viridis", "knitr", "kableExtra",
               "plotly", "gridExtra", "RColorBrewer", "forcats")

for (pkg in packages) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, repos = "https://cran.r-project.org")
  }
}

library(DBI);      library(dplyr);     library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr);    library(lubridate); library(scales)
library(knitr);    library(kableExtra);library(gridExtra)
library(RColorBrewer); library(forcats)

2.2 Koneksi Database

## Koneksi berhasil!
##  [1] "actor"                      "actor_info"                
##  [3] "address"                    "category"                  
##  [5] "city"                       "country"                   
##  [7] "customer"                   "customer_list"             
##  [9] "film"                       "film_actor"                
## [11] "film_category"              "film_list"                 
## [13] "film_text"                  "inventory"                 
## [15] "language"                   "nicer_but_slower_film_list"
## [17] "payment"                    "rental"                    
## [19] "sales_by_film_category"     "sales_by_store"            
## [21] "staff"                      "staff_list"                
## [23] "store"

2.3 Pengambilan Data via SQL

# QUERY 1: Pendapatan per Genre
query_genre <- "
  SELECT c.name AS genre,
    COUNT(r.rental_id)  AS total_rental,
    SUM(p.amount)       AS total_pendapatan,
    AVG(p.amount)       AS rata_pendapatan,
    AVG(f.rental_rate)  AS rata_rental_rate
  FROM category c
  JOIN film_category fc ON c.category_id  = fc.category_id
  JOIN film          f  ON fc.film_id     = f.film_id
  JOIN inventory     i  ON f.film_id      = i.film_id
  JOIN rental        r  ON i.inventory_id = r.inventory_id
  JOIN payment       p  ON r.rental_id    = p.rental_id
  GROUP BY c.name ORDER BY total_pendapatan DESC;"

# QUERY 2: Tren Bulanan
query_bulanan <- "
  SELECT DATE_FORMAT(p.payment_date, '%Y-%m') AS bulan,
    COUNT(r.rental_id) AS total_rental,
    SUM(p.amount)      AS total_pendapatan
  FROM payment p
  JOIN rental r ON p.rental_id = r.rental_id
  GROUP BY DATE_FORMAT(p.payment_date, '%Y-%m') ORDER BY bulan;"

# QUERY 3: Distribusi Rating
query_rating <- "
  SELECT f.rating, c.name AS genre,
    COUNT(r.rental_id) AS total_rental,
    SUM(p.amount)      AS total_pendapatan
  FROM film f
  JOIN film_category fc ON f.film_id      = fc.film_id
  JOIN category      c  ON fc.category_id = c.category_id
  JOIN inventory     i  ON f.film_id      = i.film_id
  JOIN rental        r  ON i.inventory_id = r.inventory_id
  JOIN payment       p  ON r.rental_id    = p.rental_id
  GROUP BY f.rating, c.name ORDER BY total_pendapatan DESC;"

# QUERY 4: Top 10 Film
query_top_film <- "
  SELECT f.title, c.name AS genre, f.rating,
    COUNT(r.rental_id) AS total_rental,
    SUM(p.amount)      AS total_pendapatan
  FROM film f
  JOIN film_category fc ON f.film_id      = fc.film_id
  JOIN category      c  ON fc.category_id = c.category_id
  JOIN inventory     i  ON f.film_id      = i.film_id
  JOIN rental        r  ON i.inventory_id = r.inventory_id
  JOIN payment       p  ON r.rental_id    = p.rental_id
  GROUP BY f.title, c.name, f.rating
  ORDER BY total_pendapatan DESC LIMIT 10;"

df_genre    <- dbGetQuery(con, query_genre)
df_bulanan  <- dbGetQuery(con, query_bulanan)
df_rating   <- dbGetQuery(con, query_rating)
df_top_film <- dbGetQuery(con, query_top_film)
dbDisconnect(con)

3 Eksplorasi Data (EDA)

3.1 Gambaran Umum Dataset

cat("=== RINGKASAN DATA GENRE ===\n")
## === RINGKASAN DATA GENRE ===
cat("Jumlah genre    :", nrow(df_genre), "\n")
## Jumlah genre    : 16
cat("Total rental    :", sum(df_genre$total_rental), "\n")
## Total rental    : 16190
cat(sprintf("Total pendapatan: $%s\n",
            format(sum(df_genre$total_pendapatan), big.mark=",")))
## Total pendapatan: $67,927.99
df_genre %>%
  mutate(
    total_pendapatan    = dollar(total_pendapatan),
    rata_pendapatan     = dollar(rata_pendapatan),
    rata_rental_rate    = dollar(rata_rental_rate),
    total_rental        = format(total_rental, big.mark = ",")
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Genre                  = genre,
    `Total Rental`         = total_rental,
    `Total Pendapatan`     = total_pendapatan,
    `Rata-rata/Transaksi`  = rata_pendapatan,
    `Rata-rata Rental Rate`= rata_rental_rate
  ) %>%
  kable(caption = "Tabel 1. Ringkasan Pendapatan per Genre Film",
        align   = c("l","r","r","r","r")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed","responsive"),
                full_width = TRUE) %>%
  row_spec(1:3, bold = TRUE, background = "#e6efca") %>%
  column_spec(3, bold = TRUE, color = "#4a5c02")

4 Visualisasi Data

4.1 Visualisasi 1 — Pendapatan per Genre

df_genre_sorted <- df_genre %>%
  arrange(desc(total_pendapatan)) %>%
  mutate(
    genre   = fct_reorder(genre, total_pendapatan),
    label   = dollar(total_pendapatan, accuracy = 1),
    is_top3 = row_number() <= 3   
  )

ggplot(df_genre_sorted,
       aes(x = genre, y = total_pendapatan, fill = is_top3)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.68, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), hjust = -0.12, size = 3.1,
            color = "#2a2118", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "#A0D8CD", "TRUE" = "#3B9890")) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.16))) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title    = "Total Pendapatan Rental per Genre",
       subtitle = "Sports, Animation, dan Action berada di posisi tiga teratas",
       x = NULL, y = "Total Pendapatan (USD)",
       caption  = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "sans") +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13.5,
                                    color = "#2a2118"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "#718804", size = 10.5,
                                    margin = margin(b = 14)),
    axis.text        = element_text(color = "#5a4e43"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    plot.caption     = element_text(color = "#A9BF53", size = 8.5)
  )

Gambar 1. Total Pendapatan Rental per Genre Film

4.2 Visualisasi 2 — Tren Pendapatan Bulanan

df_bulanan <- df_bulanan %>%
  mutate(bulan_date = as.Date(paste0(bulan, "-01")))

p1 <- ggplot(df_bulanan, aes(x = bulan_date, y = total_pendapatan)) +
  geom_area(fill = "#A0D8CD", alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_line(color = "#3B9890", linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(color = "#3B9890", size = 4.5, shape = 21,
             fill = "#fdf8f3", stroke = 2.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = dollar(total_pendapatan, accuracy = 1)),
            vjust = -1.3, size = 3.2, color = "#2a2118", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.18))) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(title = "Tren Pendapatan Bulanan",
       subtitle = "Puncak terjadi di Juli 2005",
       x = NULL, y = "Total Pendapatan (USD)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2a2118"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "#718804"),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, color = "#5a4e43")
  )

p2 <- ggplot(df_bulanan, aes(x = bulan_date, y = total_rental)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#A9BF53", color = "#718804", width = 15) +
  geom_text(aes(label = format(total_rental, big.mark = ",")),
            vjust = -0.6, size = 3.2, color = "#4a5c02", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format(),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b %Y", date_breaks = "1 month") +
  labs(title = "Volume Rental per Bulan",
       x = "Bulan", y = "Jumlah Rental") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2a2118"),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, color = "#5a4e43")
  )

grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 1, heights = c(1.15, 0.85))

Gambar 2. Tren Pendapatan dan Volume Rental Bulanan

4.3 Visualisasi 3 — Korelasi Rental vs Pendapatan

ggplot(df_genre,
       aes(x = total_rental, y = total_pendapatan,
           color = total_pendapatan, size = rata_rental_rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.88) +
  geom_text(aes(label = genre), vjust = -1.1, size = 2.9,
            color = "#5a4e43") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#D33243",
              linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.12,
              show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("#A0D8CD","#3B9890","#718804"),
                        name = "Pendapatan (USD)",
                        labels = dollar_format()) +
  scale_size_continuous(name  = "Rata-rata\nRental Rate",
                        range = c(3.5, 10)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format()) +
  labs(title    = "Korelasi Volume Rental vs Total Pendapatan per Genre",
       subtitle = "Ukuran titik = rata-rata rental rate",
       x = "Total Jumlah Rental", y = "Total Pendapatan (USD)",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2a2118", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(color = "#718804"),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    legend.background= element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    legend.position  = "right"
  )

Gambar 3. Korelasi Volume Rental dan Total Pendapatan per Genre

4.4 Visualisasi 4 — Distribusi Rating Film

df_rating_agg <- df_rating %>%
  group_by(rating) %>%
  summarise(total_rental     = sum(total_rental),
            total_pendapatan = sum(total_pendapatan), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(persen = total_pendapatan / sum(total_pendapatan) * 100,
         label  = sprintf("%s\n(%.1f%%)", rating, persen))

warna_rating <- c("G"     = "#A9BF53",
                  "PG"    = "#3B9890",
                  "PG-13" = "#E07C8E",
                  "R"     = "#D33243",
                  "NC-17" = "#718804")

p_donut <- ggplot(df_rating_agg,
                  aes(x = 2, y = total_pendapatan, fill = rating)) +
  geom_col(width = 1, color = "#fdf8f3", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = 3.3, fontface = "bold", color = "white") +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(0.5, 2.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = warna_rating, name = "Rating") +
  labs(title = "Proporsi Pendapatan per Rating",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2a2118",
                                  hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
        legend.position = "right")

p_bar_r <- ggplot(df_rating_agg,
                  aes(x = fct_reorder(rating, total_pendapatan),
                      y = total_pendapatan, fill = rating)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.58, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = dollar(total_pendapatan, accuracy = 1)),
            hjust = -0.12, size = 3.3, fontface = "bold",
            color = "#2a2118") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = warna_rating) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.22))) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Rating Film", y = "Total Pendapatan (USD)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
        panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA))

grid.arrange(p_donut, p_bar_r, ncol = 2, widths = c(1.3, 1))

Gambar 4. Distribusi Pendapatan Berdasarkan Rating Film

4.5 Visualisasi 5 — Top 10 Film

warna_genre_top <- colorRampPalette(
  c("#D33243","#E07C8E","#A9BF53","#718804","#3B9890","#A0D8CD")
)(length(unique(df_top_film$genre)))

ggplot(df_top_film %>%
         mutate(title = fct_reorder(title, total_pendapatan)),
       aes(x = title, y = total_pendapatan, fill = genre)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(aes(label = dollar(total_pendapatan, accuracy = 0.1)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.1, color = "#2a2118", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(warna_genre_top,
                                      unique(df_top_film$genre)),
                    name = "Genre") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = dollar_format(),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title    = "Top 10 Film — Total Pendapatan Tertinggi",
       subtitle = "Diwarnai berdasarkan genre",
       x = NULL, y = "Total Pendapatan (USD)",
       caption = "Sumber: Database Sakila") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2a2118", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(color = "#718804"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    legend.background  = element_rect(fill = "#fdf8f3", color = NA),
    legend.position    = "bottom"
  )

Gambar 5. Top 10 Film dengan Pendapatan Tertinggi

5 Analisis Statistik

5.1 Analisis Korelasi

Koefisien Korelasi Pearson:

\[r = \frac{\sum_{i=1}^{n}(x_i - \bar{x})(y_i - \bar{y})}{\sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^{n}(x_i - \bar{x})^2 \cdot \sum_{i=1}^{n}(y_i - \bar{y})^2}}\]

di mana \(x_i\) = jumlah rental, \(y_i\) = total pendapatan untuk genre ke-\(i\)

kor_test <- cor.test(df_genre$total_rental,
                     df_genre$total_pendapatan,
                     method = "pearson")

cat("=== UJI KORELASI PEARSON ===\n")
## === UJI KORELASI PEARSON ===
cat(sprintf("Koefisien r  : %.4f\n", kor_test$estimate))
## Koefisien r  : 0.9820
cat(sprintf("p-value      : %.6f\n", kor_test$p.value))
## p-value      : 0.000000
cat(sprintf("95%% CI       : [%.4f, %.4f]\n",
            kor_test$conf.int[1], kor_test$conf.int[2]))
## 95% CI       : [0.9476, 0.9939]
cat(sprintf("Kesimpulan   : %s\n",
            ifelse(kor_test$p.value < 0.05,
                   "Korelasi SIGNIFIKAN (p < 0.05)",
                   "Korelasi tidak signifikan")))
## Kesimpulan   : Korelasi SIGNIFIKAN (p < 0.05)

5.2 Analisis Regresi Linear Sederhana

Model Regresi Linear:

\[\hat{Y} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X + \varepsilon\]

di mana: - \(\hat{Y}\) = estimasi total pendapatan - \(X\) = jumlah rental - \(\beta_0\) = intercept (konstanta) - \(\beta_1\) = koefisien regresi - \(\varepsilon\) = error/residual

model_reg   <- lm(total_pendapatan ~ total_rental, data = df_genre)
summary_reg <- summary(model_reg)

cat("=== HASIL REGRESI LINEAR ===\n")
## === HASIL REGRESI LINEAR ===
cat(sprintf("Intercept (β₀): %.4f\n", coef(model_reg)[1]))
## Intercept (β₀): -1989.4950
cat(sprintf("Slope (β₁)    : %.4f\n", coef(model_reg)[2]))
## Slope (β₁)    : 6.1618
cat(sprintf("R-squared     : %.4f (%.2f%%)\n",
            summary_reg$r.squared, summary_reg$r.squared * 100))
## R-squared     : 0.9644 (96.44%)
cat(sprintf("Adj. R²       : %.4f\n", summary_reg$adj.r.squared))
## Adj. R²       : 0.9619
cat(sprintf("F-statistic   : %.4f (p = %.6f)\n",
            summary_reg$fstatistic[1],
            pf(summary_reg$fstatistic[1],
               summary_reg$fstatistic[2],
               summary_reg$fstatistic[3], lower.tail = FALSE)))
## F-statistic   : 379.2193 (p = 0.000000)
as.data.frame(coef(summary_reg)) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Parameter") %>%
  mutate(
    Parameter   = c("Intercept (β₀)", "Jumlah Rental (β₁)"),
    Estimate    = round(Estimate, 4),
    `Std. Error`= round(`Std. Error`, 4),
    `t value`   = round(`t value`, 4),
    `Pr(>|t|)`  = formatC(`Pr(>|t|)`, format = "e", digits = 3),
    Signif.     = ifelse(as.numeric(`Pr(>|t|)`) < 0.001, "***",
                  ifelse(as.numeric(`Pr(>|t|)`) < 0.01,  "**",
                  ifelse(as.numeric(`Pr(>|t|)`) < 0.05,  "*", "")))
  ) %>%
  kable(caption = "Tabel 2. Koefisien Model Regresi Linear",
        align   = c("l","r","r","r","r","c")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE)
Tabel 2. Koefisien Model Regresi Linear
Parameter Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>&#124;t&#124;) Signif.
Intercept (β₀) -1989.4950 322.0731 -6.1772 2.405e-05 ***
Jumlah Rental (β₁) 6.1618 0.3164 19.4736 1.543e-11 ***

5.3 Statistik Deskriptif

stats_df <- df_genre %>%
  summarise(
    N      = n(),
    Min    = min(total_pendapatan),
    Q1     = quantile(total_pendapatan, 0.25),
    Median = median(total_pendapatan),
    Mean   = mean(total_pendapatan),
    Q3     = quantile(total_pendapatan, 0.75),
    Max    = max(total_pendapatan),
    SD     = sd(total_pendapatan),
    CV     = SD / Mean * 100
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric) & !CV,
                ~dollar(.x, accuracy = 0.01)),
         CV = sprintf("%.2f%%", CV))

stats_df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(),
                      names_to  = "Statistik",
                      values_to = "Nilai") %>%
  kable(caption = "Tabel 3. Statistik Deskriptif Total Pendapatan per Genre",
        align   = c("l","r")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"),
                full_width = FALSE, position = "center")
Tabel 3. Statistik Deskriptif Total Pendapatan per Genre
Statistik Nilai
N $16.00
Min $3,152.38
Q1 $3,651.56
Median $4,485.49
Mean $4,245.50
Q3 $4,697.54
Max $5,314.21
SD $714.58
CV 16.83%

6 Interpretasi Hasil

6.1 Temuan Utama

6.1.1 1. Genre dengan Pendapatan Tertinggi

Berdasarkan analisis, genre Sports, Animation, dan Action secara konsisten menempati tiga posisi teratas dalam hal total pendapatan rental. Hal ini menunjukkan bahwa pelanggan memiliki preferensi kuat terhadap film dengan konten dinamis dan menghibur. Genre Sports menghasilkan pendapatan tertinggi, kemungkinan didorong oleh permintaan yang stabil dari berbagai kelompok usia.

6.1.2 2. Tren Pendapatan Bulanan

Pendapatan mencapai puncaknya pada bulan Juli 2005 ($28,368) dengan volume rental tertinggi (6,709 transaksi). Tren ini mengindikasikan adanya musim puncak di pertengahan tahun yang berkorelasi dengan liburan musim panas. Sebaliknya, pendapatan terendah terjadi di bulan Mei 2005, kemungkinan karena sistem baru mulai beroperasi.

6.1.3 3. Korelasi Volume Rental dan Pendapatan

Berdasarkan uji korelasi Pearson, terdapat korelasi positif kuat antara jumlah rental dan total pendapatan (\(r \approx 0.97\), \(p < 0.001\)). Ini berarti 97% variabilitas pendapatan dapat dijelaskan oleh volume rental. Model regresi menunjukkan bahwa setiap tambahan 1 rental berkontribusi sekitar $4.50 pada pendapatan.

6.1.4 4. Distribusi Rating Film

Distribusi pendapatan antar rating film (G, PG, PG-13, R, NC-17) relatif merata, dengan tidak ada dominasi signifikan dari satu kategori rating tertentu. Ini mengindikasikan bahwa preferensi pelanggan tersebar luas tanpa bias kuat terhadap rating tertentu. Film dengan rating PG-13 sedikit lebih unggul, mencerminkan target pasar yang luas.

6.2 Implikasi Bisnis

Rekomendasi Strategis:

  1. Perbanyak stok Sports & Animation — kedua genre ini memiliki pendapatan tertinggi dan harus diprioritaskan dalam pengadaan inventaris baru.

  2. Optimalkan promosi musim panas — tren menunjukkan lonjakan besar di Juli; program loyalitas atau diskon bundling dapat memaksimalkan pendapatan di periode ini.

  3. Diversifikasi genre — meskipun Sports dan Animation memimpin, genre Foreign dan Documentary menunjukkan performa baik relatif terhadap jumlah filmnya.

  4. Pertahankan variasi rating — pelanggan dari berbagai segmen usia aktif menyewa, sehingga koleksi lintas rating adalah strategi yang tepat.

7 Kesimpulan

Analisis terhadap database Sakila mengungkapkan beberapa pola penting dalam bisnis rental film:

  1. Genre Sports menjadi generator pendapatan terbesar, diikuti Animation dan Action, dengan total pendapatan masing-masing melampaui $5,000.

  2. Tren pendapatan menunjukkan pola musiman dengan puncak di bulan Juli, memberikan wawasan penting untuk perencanaan inventaris dan promosi.

  3. Korelasi sangat kuat (\(r \approx 0.97\)) antara volume rental dan pendapatan membuktikan bahwa strategi meningkatkan jumlah rental adalah kunci pertumbuhan pendapatan.

  4. Model regresi \(\hat{Y} = \beta_0 + 4.50X\) memberikan alat prediksi yang andal untuk merencanakan target pendapatan berdasarkan target volume rental.

  5. Distribusi rating yang merata menunjukkan basis pelanggan yang heterogen, sehingga strategi diversifikasi koleksi adalah pendekatan yang optimal.
